sanskrit:
अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च।
नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः॥
transliteration:
acchedyo 'yam adāhyo 'yam akledyo 'śoṣya eva ca
nityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur acalo 'yaṁ sanātanaḥ
literal_meaning: This (soul) is unbreakable, it cannot be burned, cannot be moistened, cannot be dried; it is eternal, all-pervading, immovable, ever-steady and primeval.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that the true Self is indestructible and immutable, countering the fear of annihilation that fuels his paralysis.
psychological_state: Existential dread dissolving into recognition of an inviolable inner essence.
trigger_scenario: A patient facing terminal illness diagnosis who equates death with total erasure.
anchor_text: You are the eternal, unchanging witness within; no harm can touch your core.
